messages.content: Always use code blocks with the appropriate language tags. If asked for a table always format it using Markdown syntax.

To convert the IP address 192.168.3.32 into binary format, you need to convert each octet separately.

The given IP address is: 192.168.3.32

Converting each octet into binary:

Octet 1 (192):
192 in binary is 11000000

Octet 2 (168):
168 in binary is 10101000

Octet 3 (3):
3 in binary is 00000011

Octet 4 (32):
32 in binary is 00100000

Combining all the binary octets together:

11000000.10101000.00000011.00100000

So, the binary equivalent of the IP address 192.168.3.32 is: 11000000.10101000.00000011.00100000.
